meta.title: Design System or Disaster System?
meta.description: The exquisite corpse between design and development.
timer.label: // time_remaining
modal.tools.title: Recommended tools
intro.rule.1: Volunteers draw each body part blindly in 3 turns
intro.rule.2: 30s per turn — press the timer to start
intro.rule.3: Fold the paper and pass it to the next volunteer without looking
intro.rule.4: Is the result a Design System or a Disaster System?
hint.head: Press the brain, eyes, ears or mouth
hint.trunk: Press the trunk, heart, arms or hands
hint.legs: Press the legs, feet or tail
slide4.subtitle.2: How do we turn it into a system?
slide4.cta.label: Share the disaster before bringing it to life:
cta.phi.title: With technic empathy
cta.phi.text: Not a Figma deliverable. It's a social contract between design, development and product.
closing.title:
Create systems
with soul
closing.p1: Like any living body, it needs constant care — communication, documentation and periodic reviews — to avoid becoming another corpse.
closing.p2: Don't work blindly. Create systems with soul and, above all... talk to each other.
closing.tools.section.label: Tools:
closing.tool.tokens-studio.desc: Synchronized design token management between Figma and code.
closing.tool.style-dictionary.desc: Automated token generation for multiple platforms.
closing.tool.axe-devtools.desc: Automated accessibility audit in the browser.
closing.tool.stark.desc: Contrast verification and color blindness simulation.
closing.tool.lighthouse.desc: Integrated performance, accessibility and SEO audit.
closing.tool.nvda.desc: Screen readers for testing the non-visual experience.
closing.tool.a11y-insights.desc: Microsoft extension for step-by-step WCAG audits.
closing.tool.i18next.desc: i18n framework with plural and context support.
closing.tool.pseudoloc.desc: Technique to detect hardcoded text and length issues.
closing.tool.chromatic.desc: Visual review of component changes with Storybook.
closing.tool.figma-tokens.desc: Token synchronization between Figma and Git repositories.
closing.tool.visbug.desc: Extension to visually edit layout in the browser.
closing.tool.touch-checker.desc: Verification of minimum touch target sizes.
closing.tool.chrome-device.desc: Mobile device and touch event emulation.
closing.tool.responsively.desc: Browser for testing multiple viewports simultaneously.
closing.tool.polypane.desc: Specialized browser for responsive and accessible development.
closing.tool.stylelint.desc: CSS linter to maintain conventions and detect errors.
closing.tool.bundlephobia.desc: npm dependency size analysis before installing.
closing.tool.knip.desc: Detection of dead code, unused exports and orphan dependencies.
closing.cta: Your Design System standing or does it need a jolt of electricity?
closing.cta.text: Publish your exquisite corpse and connect with other teams to understand how they're bringing their own creatures to life.
closing.hashtags.label: git push --tags // share the experiment:
corpse.upper: Upper part of the exquisite corpse
corpse.middle: Middle part of the exquisite corpse
corpse.lower: Lower part of the exquisite corpse
lang.btn.title: Translate to Spanish

brain.title: 🧠 Brain
brain.subtitle: Design tokens without neurons
brain.concept: Without neurons, impulses don't connect. Tokens are the system's synapses: an agreement, not a hex.

errors.desc: Tokens without semantic naming are impossible to maintain at scale. Design must define the naming convention before development implements it.

errors.errorCode:
.dark { --bg: black; }
.light { /* forgotten */ }
errors.solutionCode:
:root { --surface-1: var(--gray-100); }
.dark { --surface-1: var(--gray-900); }
errors.desc: Each token must have its counterpart in both themes, mapped from primitives.

errors.solutionCode:
padding: 16px; /* 8×2 */
margin: 8px;   /* 8×1 */
gap: 8px;      /* 8×1 */
errors.desc: Base-8 maintains vertical rhythm and alignment between components.

errors.solutionCode:
font-size: 0.875rem; /* 14/16 */
padding: 1rem;       /* relative to root */
margin: 0.5em;       /* relative to local font-size */
errors.desc: rem for global spacing, em for relation with the component.

tools.name: Tokens Studio
tools.desc: Synchronized design token management between Figma and code.

tools.name: Style Dictionary
tools.desc: Automated token generation for multiple platforms.

eyes.title: 👁️ Eyes
eyes.subtitle: Interface myopia
eyes.concept: Myopia: the interface can't see what the user needs. Contrast, focus and sharpness are the correction.

errors.errorCode:
.btn { color: #777; background: #999; }
/* Ratio: 1.8:1 — NOT WCAG compliant */
errors.solutionCode:
.btn { color: #fff; background: #1565c0; }
/* Ratio: 7:1 — Passes WCAG AAA */
errors.desc: WCAG AA requires 4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text. Design must verify contrast before delivery.

errors.errorCode:
:focus { outline: none; }
/* Broken accessibility for keyboard */
errors.desc: Keyboard users need clear focus indicators. Design must define the :focus state in the state matrix.
Without srcset, Retina screens show pixelated images. Design must export assets at @1x, @2x and @3x.
Sub-pixels cause inconsistent rendering across browsers. Decimals in Figma must not reach code.

tools.name: axe DevTools
tools.desc: Automated accessibility audit in the browser.

tools.name: Stark (Figma)
tools.desc: Contrast verification and color blindness simulation in design.

ears.title: 👂 Ears
ears.subtitle: Semantic deafness
ears.concept: Auditory/semantic accessibility. Screen readers and non-visual navigation. The system is deaf to those who cannot see.

errors.errorCode:
<button>View more</button>
<button>View more</button>
<button>View more</button>
<!-- Which one is which? -->
errors.solutionCode:
<button aria-label="View more offers">
  View more
</button>
<button aria-label="View more news">
  View more
</button>
errors.desc: Screen readers cannot distinguish buttons with identical text. Design must specify the accessible text for each component.

errors.errorCode:
Home

Section
errors.solutionCode:
Home

Section

Subsection
errors.desc: Heading jumps break screen reader navigation. Design must number heading levels in mockups.

errors.errorCode: Successfully saved
errors.solutionCode: Successfully saved
errors.desc: Without aria-live, dynamic changes are invisible to screen readers.

tools.name: NVDA
tools.desc: Free, open-source screen reader for Windows.

tools.name: VoiceOver
tools.desc: Native macOS/iOS screen reader for testing on Apple devices.

mouth.title: 👄 Mouth
mouth.subtitle: Content aphasia
mouth.concept: Aphasia cuts communication. Without flexible design for other languages, the system loses its voice.

errors.desc: German and other languages produce words up to 3× longer. Design must test with long texts.
text-overflow: ellipsis only works with white-space: nowrap. For multiline, use line-clamp.
Logical properties (inline/block) adapt automatically to RTL. Design must review layouts in both directions.

tools.name: i18next
tools.desc: Internationalization framework with plural and context support.

tools.name: Pseudolocalization
tools.desc: Technique to detect hardcoded text and length issues.

heart.title: ❤️ Heart
heart.subtitle: A heart without a soul
heart.concept: Detached component instances that do not pump blood and do not keep a system alive.

errors.desc: Detaching a component is like organ trafficking. The connection with the system is lost and it no longer receives updates.
Without an update pipeline, the DS becomes dead code. When the DS is updated but not the pages, an obsolete organ remains.
A shared glossary eliminates handoff friction. Lack of evangelism = chaos.

tools.name: Chromatic
tools.desc: Visual review of component changes with Storybook.

tools.name: Figma Tokens Plugin
tools.desc: Token synchronization between Figma and Git repositories.

trunk.title: 🦴 Trunk
trunk.subtitle: Inflexible Framework
trunk.concept: A pressure-welded structure where nothing fits by design.

errors.desc: Negative margins are patches that break in other contexts.

errors.error: Carousel with overflow hidden clipping shadows
errors.desc: overflow:hidden clips everything, leave a safety padding.

tools.name: CSS Grid Inspector
tools.desc: Grid visualization in Firefox/Chrome DevTools.

tools.name: VisBug
tools.desc: Extension to visually edit layout in the browser.

arms.title: 💪 Arms
arms.subtitle: Failed Reach
arms.concept: Floating pieces without connection; these arms reach for nothing as the layout collapses.

errors.desc: Without a scale, an infinite z-index war begins. Design must document the stacking layers.
Absolute positioning ignores flow and creates overlaps. Design must think about the layout system.

tools.name: z-index Manager
tools.desc: Stacking context layer visualization in DevTools.

tools.name: CSS Stacking Context Inspector
tools.desc: Extension to debug stacking contexts.

hands.title: 🤚 Hands
hands.subtitle: Hands of Tin
hands.concept: Clumsy and rigid, these hands cannot feel what they touch. The interface remains unresponsive when the user clicks.

errors.desc: WCAG 2.5.5 requires minimum 44×44 CSS px of touch area. Design must respect minimums in the spec.
Decorative pseudo-elements should let interaction events pass through.

errors.errorCode:
/* Modal open but body
   still scrolls behind */
errors.desc: Scrolling behind the modal is disorienting and breaks the user experience.

tools.name: Touch Target Checker
tools.desc: Verification of minimum touch target sizes.

tools.name: Chrome Device Mode
tools.desc: Mobile device and touch event emulation.

legs.title: 🦿 Legs
legs.subtitle: Static Limbs
legs.concept: Rigidity prevents all progress; responsive design isn't about stitched patches, it's about native fluidity.

errors.desc: Fixed sizes break on any different screen. Design must deliver layouts with min/max.
100vh does not account for the mobile browser bar. dvh does. Always include a fallback.
Without safe-area, content ends up behind the notch or home indicator.

errors.errorCode:
.col { float: left; width: 33.33%; }
.clearfix::after { clear: both; }
errors.desc: Floats for layout is a 2010 technique. Grid and Flex are the standard.

tools.name: Responsively
tools.desc: Browser for testing multiple viewports simultaneously.

tools.name: Polypane
tools.desc: Specialized browser for responsive and accessible development.

feet.title: 🦶 Feet
feet.subtitle: Unstable Footing
feet.concept: Claws should ground the system; without a pipeline and audits, every release loses its grip.

errors.desc: Deep selectors create coupling and specificity wars.
Small, well-named files with centralized tokens.
Outdated dependencies are security holes and dead weight.

errors.solutionCode:
/* Quarterly:
   - Lighthouse performance
   - axe-core accessibility
   - Bundle size analysis
   - DS coverage report */
errors.desc: What is not measured cannot be improved. Regular audits = product health.

tools.name: Storybook
tools.desc: Isolated component documentation and development.

tools.name: Lighthouse
tools.desc: Integrated performance, accessibility and SEO audit in Chrome.

tail.title: 🦎 Tail
tail.subtitle: The legacy burden
tail.concept: The lizard's tail that falls off due to technical debt.

errors.error: External libraries for a single element
errors.desc: Every KB of JS has a parse cost. Use native APIs first.
Dead code confuses, weighs and gives a false sense of complexity.
A DS without documentation or tests depends on people, not processes.

tools.name: Bundlephobia
tools.desc: npm dependency size analysis before installing.

tools.name: Knip
tools.desc: Detection of dead code, unused exports and orphan dependencies.

slide4.subtitle.post.2: Share your exquisite corpse and prove whether it is a Design System or a Disaster System.
visual.demo.themes.label.error: Dark text on dark bg = invisible
visual.demo.negative.label.error: Patch that breaks in another context
visual.demo.lineclamp.label.error: Abrupt cut in middle of sentence
